## Environment & Commands (CRITICAL — survives compaction)

**Project**: ark-asa — lean self-hosted ARK: Survival Ascended server (Docker + GE-Proton)
**Package Manager**: n/a (Docker)
**Container**: yes — `docker compose --env-file .env.test up` (test) / `.env.prod` (prod/VPS)
**Database**: none yet (MySQL arrives in M2 for the shared store)

**Test box**: `dell` (10.1.4.4, bare-metal Ubuntu 22.04, 8 cores, 64GB) at `~/ark-asa-server`.
SSH in to operate; the repo is a clone of this one (push to GitHub to sync both ways).

```bash
# Boot (reads ./.env — currently the test profile). First boot installs ~13GB once.
docker compose up --build
# Config loop: edit ./config/GameUserSettings.ini, then (fast boot, ~20s, no re-download)
docker compose restart the-island
# Debug a crash: surface the real Wine fault instead of -all
WINEDEBUG=+err,+seh docker compose up
# Stop
docker compose down
```

**VERIFIED WORKING on dell (2026-06-20)**: `Server "ARK-Test" has successfully started!` →
`Full Startup: ~20s` → `advertising for join (10.33GB Mem)`, port 7777/udp bound. Restart loop
confirmed fast-boot (skips Steam).

**HOST PREREQ**: `vm.max_map_count >= 262144` — auto-applied by the compose `sysctl` service.

---

## Active Decisions (append with WHY)

- [2026-06-21] **M2 shared-economy store — DONE pending final flip; modded ArkShop economy verified LIVE on dell**: `ENABLE_ASAAPI=1` launches AsaApiLoader → AsaApi 1.21 + ArkShop V1.4 + Permissions V1.1 load; ArkShop connects to MariaDB (`mariadb:11.4`, `mariadb:3306` service name, `mysql_native_password`) and creates its schema; RCON `SetPoints <eosid> <amount>` persists a row (verified Points 0→250 + in-game); edit-on-host plugin config survives restart; `ENABLE_ASAAPI=0` = byte-for-byte M1 vanilla rollback. All 6 Phase-5 ACs + 22/22 cumulative ACs MET; full cumulative sweep all-green. Plugin config = host bind `./plugins-config/<Plugin>/config.json` (entrypoint symlinks the FILE only, re-injects the `.Mysql` block from `.env` each boot). ASA API Utils mod **955333** auto-appended to `-mods` when modded.
- [2026-06-21] **Two runtime bugs the dell gate caught (not the static reviewers)**: (1) `setup_plugin_configs` symlinked the whole plugin dir → deleted `ArkShop.dll` ("Plugin does not exist"); fix = symlink only `config.json`. (2) Modded server crash-looped on `docker compose restart` — stale `/tmp` Xvfb lock+socket raced `kill -0` into a false-pass → loader launched against a dead display; fix = `rm -f /tmp/.X0-lock /tmp/.X11-unix/X0` before Xvfb. Lesson: the config-loop `restart` path MUST be runtime-tested, not just `up -d`.
- [2026-06-21] **deploy_plugins derives its root-artifact set from `/opt/asaapi/*`** (not hand-listed rm+cp): a version bump that adds/drops a root DLL is picked up automatically; entrypoint can't drift from the Dockerfile bake. (Closed M2 cumulative code-review concern #1.)
- [2026-06-21] **dell operational facts**: DataGrip → ark-asa DB via gitignored `docker-compose.override.yml` publishing `mariadb` on host port **3307** (3306 is taken by an unrelated `mifi-mysql` MySQL-8.4 container — THAT was the source of the `sha256_password` DataGrip error, not the ark-asa DB). Use the **MariaDB** driver. ArkShop UI = MX-E Ark Shop UI / "official ArkShopUI Ascended" = CurseForge mod **942249**; dell `.env` `MODS=955333,942249,928597,947033` (API Utils 955333 moved FIRST — experiment to fix UI; entrypoint normally auto-appends it LAST). Committed compose stays internal-only (no host port) for prod. **ArkShop UI open-key is CLIENT-SIDE, customizable F1–F12 within the mod — NO server config file sets it** (ArkShop config.json has no UiKey by design; "zero config" per docs). `/shop` in chat returns the TEXT list (native ArkShop); the graphical MX-E overlay is a separate client hotkey. UNRESOLVED: F3 + `/shop` show text, not the graphical UI — server-side all-correct (ArkShop loaded, 955333 valid + no "singleton not found", UI mod downloaded to client); likely client keybind/version-compat → MX-E Discord (discord.gg/u5v44xC). `944227` (Auto Run) = client-only mod, must NOT be in server `MODS` (caused a fatal "mods not installed" crash-loop).
- [2026-06-21] **M2 MERGED to `main`** (`c46fa66`): main's `config/Game.ini` + `config/GameUserSettings.ini` (tracked on origin/main) are PRESERVED through the merge — the feature branch never tracked them; verified byte-identical post-merge. M2 plan folder flips to `plans/done/` on next radar rebuild.
- [2026-06-20] **Image = immutable stack only; game installs at runtime onto a volume**: per `.claude/rules/build-time-vs-runtime.md`. Baking the ~30GB game into the image would force a rebuild every ASA patch. Image holds SteamCMD/GE-Proton/rcon/tini; game + Proton prefix live on the `ark-game` volume, installed by the entrypoint (skip-validate after first install = fast boot).
- [2026-06-20] **Prod/test env profiles; BattlEye is a toggle**: `.env.test` = fast boot + instant kill + anti-cheat OFF; `.env.prod` = update-on-boot + SaveWorld + BattlEye ON. Splitting them caught that the entrypoint had `-NoBattlEye` hardcoded — prod would otherwise have shipped a cheatable PvP server. (This is the env note Patrick asked to record.)
- [2026-06-20] **M1 single-server: no shared volumes yet**: sharing only earns its keep with a 2nd consumer. `steam` / cluster / MySQL / shared-config sharing arrives additively in M2/M3 (per-server game volume + shared steam + shared cluster + MySQL) — no teardown. Avoids speculative single-consumer "shared" volumes.
- [2026-06-20] **Host requires `vm.max_map_count >= 262144`**: ASA exceeds the Linux default (65530) → exit-code-21 crash-loop ~1s after launch, before map load. Non-namespaced kernel param, can't be set in-container — so a privileged `sysctl` init service in compose writes it to the HOST kernel before the server boots, automatically on every host (WSL + VPS). Manual `/etc/sysctl.conf` is the fallback if a host blocks privileged containers.
- [2026-06-20] **THE exit-21 root cause was missing `steamclient.so`, NOT WSL2**: Proton's `lsteamclient` loads the native Steam client from `~/.steam/sdk{64,32}/steamclient.so` at launch; without it the server asserts in `steamclient_main.c:375` and aborts (exit 21), right after Sentry init. The `parkervcp/steamcmd:proton` base does NOT bundle it. Fix: Dockerfile runs `steamcmd +login anonymous +quit` at build to bake `linux{32,64}/steamclient.so` into the image; entrypoint symlinks `~/.steam/sdk{64,32}/steamclient.so` to them each boot (`~/.steam` is ephemeral, not on the volume). **WSL2 was exonerated** — the crash reproduced identically on bare-metal dell with a complete install + correct max_map_count. (`WINEDEBUG=+err,+seh` surfaced the assertion; `-all` hid it.)
- [2026-06-20] **Config bind is SHALLOW + symlinked, not a deep bind**: binding `./config` directly into `…/ShooterGame/Saved/Config/WindowsServer` made Docker root-create the volume's intermediate dirs, blocking the non-root `container` user from writing `Logs`/saves (silent first-boot crash on a fresh volume). Now: bind `./config` → `/home/container/config` (shallow), entrypoint creates the dir chain as the container user and symlinks `WindowsServer` → the host mount. Edit-on-host loop preserved; ASA writes `GameUserSettings.ini` back through the link (verified).
- [2026-06-20] **ASA needs the Windows depot + a trustworthy install marker**: app 2430930 ships Windows-only, so steamcmd on Linux needs `+@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType windows` before `+login` or it fails with "Missing configuration". And steamcmd exits 0 even when `app_update` fails, so the `.installed` marker is gated on the server exe actually existing — otherwise a partial download falsely marks "installed", fast-boot skips repair, and the server crashes.
- [2026-06-21] **AsaApi loads under GE-Proton — M2 core unknown ANSWERED (Phase 4, commit 4f19274)**: launch flips `ArkAscendedServer.exe` → `AsaApiLoader.exe` behind `ENABLE_ASAAPI` (1=modded default, 0=byte-for-byte vanilla rollback, no rebuild). Proven on dell: AsaApi 1.21 + ArkShop V1.4 + Permissions V1.1 load, server advertises for join. THREE non-obvious requirements found only by the first real build/boot — (1) the `steamcmd:proton` base lacks `unzip` (added apt layer; Phase 2's static probe ran unzip on the host, not in-image — graveyard candidate); (2) `AsaApiLoader.exe` creates a Win32 window so it needs a real X display (Xvfb in the loader branch, socket+`kill -0` liveness guarded) — vanilla stays headless via SDL dummy; (3) **AsaApi requires `ArkAscendedServer.pdb`** (SHA-256'd to key its symbol-offset cache) — the M1 pdb-shed optimization broke it → keep pdb when modded + self-healing `ensure_modded_pdb()` (steamcmd-validate restore, size-floored). `WINEDEBUG=+err,+seh` surfaced the Xvfb fault.
- [2026-06-21] **ASA API Utils CurseForge mod ID = 955333**: discovered via ArkShop's optional-mod warning during Phase 4. Needed for Phase 5 (`MODS=` → `-mods=`); without it ArkShop logs "AsaApiUtils singleton not found" and falls back to default messaging.

## Project-Wide Notes

*(cross-workstream context, gotchas, user preferences not tied to one plan)*

- **exit-21 is a generic ASA early-abort** — it has *multiple* causes (too-low `vm.max_map_count`, missing `steamclient.so`, partial install). Both the map_count and steamclient causes are now handled in-image/compose. When it recurs, `WINEDEBUG=+err,+seh` is the move — it names the real Wine fault that `-all` hides.
- **WSL2 client join**: enable `networkingMode=mirrored` in `.wslconfig` to join from the Windows ARK client; logs/RCON work regardless. Direct-connect via console `open localhost:7777`.
- **Informal roadmap**: M1 lean fast image (current) → M2 AsaApi + ArkShop + MySQL **shared store** (the thing Nitrado can't do — needs a real `/plan` + an ADR for the shared-economy schema) → M3 cluster (store shared across maps) → M4 config tooling / backups / TS CLI → VPS deploy. Real PvP server lives on a VPS; WSL stays the config sandbox.
- **Process note**: M1 was built as a fast casual slice (no formal `/plan`), so the plan's Documentation-Impact step was skipped — acceptable at M1's "small tool → README" doc tier. M2 is multi-service + has hard-to-reverse schema decisions → run full `/plan` (incl. docs step + ADR) before building it.

- **PLANNING IN PROGRESS (2026-06-20): M2 `m2-shared-economy-store`** (initiative child of `ark-asa-server`). Research done; awaiting Patrick's OK on a 5-phase shape, then writing `.claude/plans/active/ark-asa-server/m2-shared-economy-store/plan.md` + plan-reviewer. Key research findings:
  - **AsaApi pinned = v1.21** (Apr 2026). Installs to `ShooterGame/Binaries/Win64`; plugins → `Win64/ArkApi/Plugins/<name>/` (DLL name must match folder); log at `ShooterGame/Win64/logs/ArkApi.log`. Launch `AsaApiLoader.exe` w/ same params as `ArkAscendedServer.exe`. **Requires MS VC++ 2019 redist.**
  - **Two build-vs-runtime tensions, both resolved the same rule-faithful way** (prefix AND `Win64` live on the `ark-game` volume, so by the rule's 3-question test → runtime/entrypoint, not Dockerfile): (a) **VC++ redist** installs in the container at runtime (bake the `.exe` in `/opt`, entrypoint runs it into the volume prefix, marker-guarded) — Patrick's explicit call, own phase; (b) **plugins** bake to `/opt/asaapi/` in the image (pinned) and the entrypoint syncs them onto the volume's `Win64` each boot (same pattern as `steamclient.so`). Phase 3 also **amends `build-time-vs-runtime.md`** so its table matches the volume-backed-prefix reality.
  - **ArkShop** deps: **Permissions** plugin (baked) + **ASA API Utils** CurseForge *mod* (rides existing `MODS=` → needs its mod ID at exec; absence = `Singleton not found`). Config `Mysql` block: `UseMysql/MysqlHost/User/Pass/DB/Port`. **MySQL 8.0.28+ rejected; MariaDB any version** → propose `mariadb:11.4` LTS.
  - Proposed phases: 1 MariaDB svc+secrets · 2 bake plugins (/opt + sync) · 3 VC++ in container (+rule amend) · 4 flip→AsaApiLoader (AsaApi loads = HIGH-risk gate) · 5 ArkShop↔MariaDB end-to-end (HIGH). Plugin-binary distribution channel (GitHub releases vs auth-gated ark-server-api.com) = open exec risk for Phase 2.
- **WHERE WE LEFT OFF (2026-06-20)**: **M1 DONE — server boots and advertises on `dell`.** The exit-21 blocker was NOT WSL2 (exonerated): it was missing `steamclient.so` for Proton's lsteamclient (see Active Decisions). Four real bugs fixed this session: (1) missing steamclient.so → bake at build + symlink at boot; (2) deep config bind root-owning the volume → shallow bind + symlink; (3) missing `+@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType windows` → "Missing configuration"; (4) install marker trusting steamcmd's lying exit code → gate on the exe existing. All four are in Dockerfile/entrypoint/compose. Verified: full startup ~20s, advertising, port bound, fast-boot restart loop works, config writes through to host `./config`.
  - **Image ownership / leanness (RESOLVED)**: keeping `FROM parkervcp/steamcmd:proton` as-is. Patrick cares about RAM/CPU, not image MB — a bookworm-slim/from-scratch base only trims disk, not runtime RAM/CPU (the cost is GE-Proton + Wine + the game, identical regardless of base). Not worth the rebuild.
  - **NEXT (M2)**: AsaApi + ArkShop + MySQL shared store — run full `/plan` + ADR before building (multi-service, hard-to-reverse schema). When AsaApi lands, launch `AsaApiLoader.exe` (not `ArkAscendedServer.exe`) per `build-time-vs-runtime.md`.
  - **Not yet committed**: the 4 fixes are in the working tree on both boxes (uncommitted). Commit when ready. README still has the old "30GB" figure (real install ~13GB) — refresh on next pass.
- **WHERE WE LEFT OFF (2026-06-21)**: **M2 shipped + merged to main** (shared economy store live on `dell`). M3 (cluster) is NEXT. Repo-hygiene pass this session: stray `phase5-runtime-evidence.md` moved into the M2 plan folder; roadmap M2 marker fixed to ✅ DONE / `(status: shipped)` + M3 tagged NEXT; `.gitignore` now covers `docker-compose.override.yml`; dell's live `.env` mods section de-crufted (dead `# MODS` line + Phase-4 header removed, values unchanged). M2 plan folder intentionally stays under `active/` — the initiative folder only flips to `done/` atomically when M3 + roadmap are also done.
  - **Hook gotcha**: to edit the initiative `roadmap.md`, claim a *child* plan slug (e.g. `m2-shared-economy-store`), not the roadmap slug — the read-block resolves ownership via `{slug}/plan.md`, which the roadmap-slug claim lacks.
- **IN PROGRESS (2026-06-21): supply-crate loot redesign** (new workstream, NOT M2/M3; brainstorming). Decisions so far: author full custom `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems` tables in `config/Game.ini`, **ALL-MAPS in one file** (ASA silently ignores crate class strings absent on the loaded map), with the **same loot design per color tier replicated across every map's matching class string** ("same per map"). **Ring drops = vanilla "Double" crate variants** (separate class strings) → tune to slightly-better-and-more than the non-ring of the same color. Goal: more OP than vanilla but NOT instant-endgame → rare items (top dino-saddle BPs, ascendant gear) ride on low drop weights, not guaranteed slots. Currently iterating the 6-rung white→red tier ladder (what each color holds). **Open decisions**: (a) neutralize `SupplyCrateLootQualityMultiplier` (Game.ini line 34, currently 2.5) → 1.0 so authored table quality = real quality [recommended]; `FishingLootQualityMultiplier` is a separate system, leave it. (b) every crate + item class string must be verified EXACT at author time — a typo = silent no-op (crate drops nothing / stays vanilla, no error).
  - **Locked design rules (2026-06-21)**: (1) `SupplyCrateLootQualityMultiplier` → 1.0 commented (quality authored in tables); `FishingLootQualityMultiplier` untouched. (2) **BP-at-half**: every gear item (tools/weapons/armor/saddles ONLY) gets a second blueprint entry at HALF the item's weight (BP appears ~half as often as the item). Structures/turrets/kibble/resources/ammo = item-only, no BP (no quality tier / no BP exists). (3) **BP quality matches the item's tier quality** (mastercraft sword → mastercraft sword BP) — one quality band per tier, item + BP both roll in it. (4) **Kibble: white→green** (white = humblest rung). (5) **Ring (=vanilla Double) delta** (proposed, pending confirm): +1 extra item-set rolled + quality nudged ~20% — deliberately less than vanilla's flat 2×.
  - **6-rung ladder (drafted, awaiting Patrick markup)**: ⚪White(3) Primitive→Ramshackle: metal structures + basic pick/hatchet/spear/bow(+BP) · 🟢Green(15) Ramshackle→Apprentice: kibble + tools/bow/pike/armor(+BP) · 🔵Blue(25) Apprentice→Journeyman: journeyman tools/crossbow/longneck/pistol/chitin-flak(+BP) + 1 heavy turret + common saddles(+BP) · 🟣Purple(35) Journeyman→Mastercraft: mastercraft tools/rifle/shotgun/flak(+BP) + 1-2 turrets + mid saddles(+BP) · 🟡Yellow(45) Mastercraft→low Ascendant: ascendant pick/hatchet + mc-asc guns/riot(+BP) + high saddles rex/spino/theri(+BP) · 🔴Red(60) Ascendant: ascendant gear across board(+BP) + ALL top saddles giga/wyvern at low weight(+BP) + turret/ammo stacks. **Tek resolved (2026-06-21)**: tek engrams stay FREE (`bAutoUnlockAllEngrams=true` unchanged — Patrick's call); NO tek BPs in drops (pointless — engrams already unlocked; the real tek gate is ELEMENT, not engrams). Red includes a SMALL element boost only — "enough to boost a start, not skip the grind" (Patrick first said ~150 but tempered to "a bit, not a lot"; propose ~25 red / ~50 red+ring at low weight, confirm). Blue uses AUTO turret (not heavy); heavy turrets start at purple. **Element qty RESOLVED**: ~150 red / ~200 red+ring, low-weight bonus set — Patrick's bosses are boosted 3× (Game.ini lines 198-233: Dragon Alpha 1320, KingTitan Alpha 1500), so 150 is a modest head-start vs a 600-1500 boss haul, not a skip. **Structure CONFIRMED (verified vs syntax docs, not guessed)**: `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems` `ItemSets` is an ARRAY — multiple item-sets per crate; `MinItemSets`/`MaxItemSets` = how many sets fire, each set has own `MinNumItems`/`MaxNumItems`/`SetWeight`/`bItemsRandomWithoutReplacement`. Chosen model: per tier = guaranteed **Gear set** (tools/weapons/armor +BPs, pulls N) + **bonus sets** (Saddles, Misc=turret/structure/element) with `MinNumItems=0` so they're a chance, gear is reliable. Also noted: `bAppendItemSets=true` can ADD to vanilla without erasing (not used — we full-override). **Pull-count ladder — Patrick edited 2026-06-21 to make high tiers RARER** (GEAR pull, non-ring/ring): White 1-2/3-4 · Green 1-2/3-4 · Blue 1-2/3-4 · Purple 1-2/3-4 · Yellow 1-2/3 · **Red 1/2** (red pulls just ONE gear item → any specific ascendant piece is rare). Ring delta now NON-uniform (white ring +2 items, red ring +1) — Patrick to confirm intentional. **"Ascendant way more rare" plan**: (1) red quality curve so ascendant is the top-edge tail not default; (2) lowest weight on crown items (giga saddle, ascendant gear). **Gear = tools+weapons+armor in one set** (so "Gear pulled" covers all three; weapons weighted < tools — keep); **Saddles + Misc(turret/structure/element/ammo) = separate bonus sets** (`MinNumItems=0`). Proposed: expand summary table to show Gear/Saddle/Misc pull columns (was gear-only — caused confusion). Awaiting Patrick confirm on saddle/misc pull counts + ring-non-uniform, then full per-tier table rewrite. **Design doc is the SoT — REVISED 2026-06-21 (commit cce5757) to the 4-GROUP model** (Gear guaranteed/growing-menu/+BP · Saddles bonus blue+/master-set/+BP · Turrets bonus · Resources guaranteed≥2 incl ammo+kibble, black pearls purple+, element red) with quality-as-TIER-property + ascendant=rare-tail. **This doc SUPERSEDES the inline ladder/group notes above in this bullet** — read `docs/internal/design/supply-crate-loot-design.md`, don't trust the older inline detail. Awaiting Patrick's final markup → then /plan. Author-time: verify EVERY crate+item+saddle class string per map (typo = silent no-op) + map quality bands to numeric MinQuality/MaxQuality + the ascendant-rare curve.
  - **NOT-YET-IN-DOC decisions (2026-06-21, pending next rewrite — capture so compaction doesn't lose them)**: (1) **Quality gating extends to ALL gear** (not just tools): cheap items can roll high quality early, powerful items gated to later tiers AND capped low when they first appear. (2) **Structures = 5th group** (Gear/Saddles/Turrets/Resources/**Structures**), bonus **base-kit pull 0–3** (foundations+walls+ceilings+doors+dino gates+behemoth gates so a hit = buildable base); **stone at white/green → metal at blue+** (behemoth gates metal/blue+); item-only. (3) **Stone vs metal TOOL dual-track**: Stone pick/hatchet white→blue (≤Mastercraft, blue ≤Ascendant, then phase out); Metal pick/hatchet/**sickle** blue+ only, quality capped App–Journey→Ascendant climbing (no stone sickle exists → sickle is metal-track). (4) **Saddles → WEIGHT BANDS by creature power** (not a fixed list): Common~10 / Mid~5 / High~3 / Crown~1 / **Tek~1 (RED-ONLY)**; added Tusoteuthis + Dunkleosteus; full saddleable roster verified at impl, DROP bareback creatures (wyvern/managarmr/griffin/shadowmane have NO saddle — earlier list had errors). (5) Tek saddles = red-only, lowest weight (need element to craft anyway). **RESOLVED + NOW IN DOC (commit edcccc8, v3)**: mystery dino = **Velonasaur** (the spike-spinner, Mid band); doc rewritten to **5-group** (Gear/Saddles/Turrets/Resources/Structures); saddles use power-bands (Common/Mid/High/Crown/Tek-red-only) with the **full saddleable roster auto-pulled from Beacon at implementation** (NOT hand-typed — Patrick's own wiki dump had the saddle column wrong, marking Parasaur/Spino as bareback, proving manual lists are error-prone). Tuso=High, Dunkleosteus+Velonasaur=Mid. **Current doc = `docs/internal/design/supply-crate-loot-design.md` @ edcccc8 (v3, 5-group) — THE SoT.** **Design APPROVED by Patrick 2026-06-21** (full saddle enumeration in commit db6693d). **DECISION: NO formal /plan** (Patrick's call — it's content in ONE file; design doc IS the contract). **EXECUTING via lean 3-step path**: (1) verify class strings (crate classes + item/saddle/resource/structure classes — wrong string = silent no-op, the only real risk); (2) author `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems` block into `config/Game.ini`; (3) boot-test on dell (pop a crate, confirm drops match the table). **Scope: ALL currently-released ASA maps** (Patrick 2026-06-21 — NOT just Island/Center; the all-maps-one-file design, absent classes silently ignored). Each map has its own crate-class suffix (e.g. `_TheCenter_C`); same loot tables stamped across every map's classes. Need to (a) verify the current released-map roster — date-sensitive, past Jan-2026 cutoff, VERIFY don't guess — and (b) pull each map's crate-class pattern from Beacon. dell currently loads TheCenter_WP so that's the boot-test map, but the file carries all maps. SoT design doc: `docs/internal/design/supply-crate-loot-design.md` @ ee29f89.
  - **CLASS-STRING VERIFICATION (2026-06-21)**: (a) **Game files CANNOT be grepped** for crate/item class names — ASA IoStore (.ucas) stores FName fragments SPLIT ("SupplyCrate" separate from "_Level60_TheCenter"), assembled at runtime; grep of dell's `ShooterGame/Content/Paks` (9.8G) only yields generic tokens. Don't retry grep — it's a confirmed dead end. (b) **Beacon (usebeacon.app) is the SoT for class strings** (proper ASA extraction). TheCenter confirmed: 6 beacons `SupplyCrate_Level03/15/25/35/45/60_TheCenter_C` (red confirmed via live Beacon URL — `_TheCenter_C` suffix is REAL, not the suffix-less Island form a forum digest wrongly claimed); cave crates `SupplyCrate_Cave_QualityTier1/2/3/4_TheCenter_C`. (c) **OPEN: ring/"Double" variant name** (`_Double_TheCenter_C`? vs `_TheCenter_Double_C`? vs `_Double_C`?) — unresolved; guess = silent no-op. Resolve via in-game `cheat summon` test on dell OR validate at boot-test. **Awaiting Patrick**: do in-game summon check, or author against confirmed `_TheCenter_C` pattern + verify at boot.
  - **BUILD APPROACH DECIDED (2026-06-21): use BEACON (usebeacon.app, free) to generate the config** — Patrick's call. Rationale: 8 released ASA maps (Island, Scorched, Center, Aberration, Extinction, Astraeos, Ragnarok, Valguero; Genesis1 drops Jul 2 2026), each with own crate classes; Aberration (zone crates) + Extinction (Orbital Supply Drops) are DIFFERENT loot systems not suffix-swaps; class strings can't be grepped (IoStore) or reliably web-scraped. Beacon's "define once → apply to all maps" auto-resolves every map's correct classes incl. the oddball ones. **My role**: design (DONE, approved doc) + Beacon-translation guidance + dell boot-test. **Design→Beacon mapping**: 5 groups→Item Sets; items+weights→Item Set Entries; quality bands→Min/Max Quality; **BP-at-half → entry "Chance to be Blueprint"=33%** (BP:item=1:2); Resources min-items 2 / Structures min0-max3; Ring=Double loot sources +1 item +~20% quality. Workflow: Beacon config → Maps=all 8 → build sets per doc → Export Game.ini → paste into config/Game.ini + SupplyCrateLootQualityMultiplier=1.0 → commit → boot-test on dell (TheCenter). **SEED-FILE APPROACH (2026-06-21, Patrick — no manual entry)**: Beacon IMPORTS a Game.ini (parses `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems`). Plan: **I write a GENERATOR script** (e.g. `tools/gen-loot.py`) that expands the design doc into a seed Game.ini (all groups/tiers/items/weights/quality/BP-chance/ring) against **Island base beacon classes** (Beacon's canonical loot sources). Patrick imports seed → Beacon → "apply to all 8 maps" (Beacon resolves per-map class strings incl. Aberration zone-crates/Extinction OSDs — the part I can't do) → Export Game.ini → paste → commit → boot-test dell. Generator = reproducible/tweakable (change design → regen → re-import). **Risk mitigated**: seed needs exact ITEM class strings (~50) I can't grep — but Beacon flags unrecognized items LOUDLY on import (not silent). **BREAKTHROUGH (2026-06-21): pulled Beacon's content DB directly — NO GUI needed.** Beacon desktop installed on Windows (`/mnt/c/Users/patri/AppData/Roaming/The ZAZ/Beacon/Libraries/ArkSA.sqlite`, 40MB, read via WSL+python3; copy .sqlite+-wal+-shm to /tmp to apply WAL). Extracted EVERYTHING: `loot_containers` (129 crate class strings + per-map `availability` bitmask + min/max sets) and `engrams` (3897 item class strings by label). **Full Beacon ASA DB snapshotted to repo as a reference library** `docs/internal/reference/beacon-asa/` (ee37d9e — 15 TSV tables incl creatures/engrams/loot_containers/ini_options/spawn_points/colors + README w/ map-mask key; ~7MB text). THE local home for all ARK class-string/config data → no more web research/guessing for any future ARK task (loot, spawns, stats, breeding, colors). Generator reads `loot_containers.tsv` + `engrams.tsv` from here. Map mask: Island=1 Scorched=2 Center=4 Aberration=8 Extinction=16 Astraeos=32 Ragnarok=64 Valguero=128 LostColony=256(excluded-unreleased). **Ring naming CONFIRMED**: `SupplyCrate_Level##_Double_<Map>_C`. Per-map reality is irregular (shared classes, Val_ prefix, Scorched 03/15/30/45/55/70, Aberration 10/25/35/50/65/80 + surface/cave/dungeon, Extinction = Orbital Supply Drops not sky beacons) — every container carries a color LABEL ("Island White", "Aberration Surface Blue", "Extinction Orbital Hard", "...Bonus"=ring) so generator maps each→tier by label. **SCOPE = all lootable crates** (Patrick): beacons + all caves + OSDs + Ab dungeons + Scorched buried treasure + Ragnarok treasure; EXCLUDE event hats/gifts + resource nodes (beaver dams, volcano rock, Dragon Hoard, admin test). **NEXT: build `tools/gen-loot.py`** — reads the 2 TSVs, resolves item classes by exact label (FAIL LOUD on unresolved), maps crates→tier by label, emits full `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems` for config/Game.ini. Baked-in defaults to confirm: tier→quality numbers (tunable, verify at boot); OSD Easy→blue/Medium→purple/Hard→yellow/Legendary→red; Astraeos "Lime"(Lvl90)→red.
  - **GENERATOR BUILT + WORKING (2026-06-21, commit e60a0f4)**: `tools/gen-loot.ts` + `tools/loot-design.ts` (Bun TS, seed of M4 CLI; chose TS over Py to avoid build-twice). Reads design + Beacon TSV snapshots → resolves items by exact label (FAILS LOUD; already caught ~28 bad names + bareback creatures wrongly banded: Aurochs/Moschops/Diplocaulus/Anglerfish/Acrocanthosaurus/Megachelon/Astrocetus removed; Beacon uses Carno/Trike/Stego/Mosasaur/Quetz/Bronto/Paracer/Carcharo, Metal Sword/Sickle, Heavy Turret, "... Ceiling & Hatchframe", "Stone/Metal Gateway") → maps each crate→tier by Beacon label → emits `ConfigOverrideSupplyCrateItems`. **Run: `cd tools && bun run gen-loot.ts`** → `tools/out/game-ini-loot.txt` (gitignored, regenerable) + `tools/out/crate-map-report.txt`. Result: **111 crates across all 8 maps, 0 unmapped, all items resolved**, 12 junk excluded (events/nodes), 264 non-crate containers skipped. Tek band uses explicit labels (Tek Rex Saddle vs Mosasaur Tek Saddle — inconsistent Beacon naming). **NEXT**: Patrick reviews tier mapping (crate-map-report) → integrate block into config/Game.ini → boot-test on dell (TheCenter): pop crates, confirm loot matches. Tunable knobs all in loot-design.ts (weights/quality/quantities/pulls).
